---
title: "References and Citation Strategy"
nocite: |
  @*
---

## Citation Strategy

The course cites sources for four distinct purposes:

1. **Foundations** for probability, Bayesian theory, and decision theory.
2. **Methodological landmarks** for Monte Carlo, MCMC, HMC, variational inference, and Bayesian workflow.
3. **Modeling references** for hierarchical models, Gaussian processes, nonparametrics, and predictive evaluation.
4. **Software references** for `R`, `Stan`, `cmdstanr`, `posterior`, `bayesplot`, `loo`, and `JAX`.

## Linked Reference Guide

The bibliography below remains the formal citation record. This section is the practical reading guide: each item links to a publisher page, official documentation page, DOI landing page, or open-access version where possible.

### Core Texts

- [Bayesian Data Analysis](https://www.routledge.com/Bayesian-Data-Analysis-Third-Edition/Gelman-Carlin-Stern-Dunson-Vehtari-Rubin/p/book/9781439840955)
- [Monte Carlo Statistical Methods](https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-1-4757-4145-2)
- [Probabilistic Machine Learning: Advanced Topics](https://mitpress.mit.edu/9780262048439/probabilistic-machine-learning)

### Core Method Papers

- [The No-U-Turn Sampler](https://jmlr.org/papers/v15/hoffman14a.html)
- [A Conceptual Introduction to Hamiltonian Monte Carlo](https://arxiv.org/abs/1701.02434)
- [Variational Inference: A Review for Statisticians](https://doi.org/10.1080/01621459.2017.1285773)
- [Practical Bayesian Model Evaluation Using Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation and WAIC](https://doi.org/10.1007/s11222-016-9696-4)
- [Rank-Normalization, Folding, and Localization: An Improved R-hat](https://doi.org/10.1214/20-BA1221)
- [Visualization in Bayesian Workflow](https://doi.org/10.1111/rssa.12378)

### Software And Documentation

- [R Project](https://www.r-project.org/)
- [Stan](https://mc-stan.org/)
- [CmdStanR](https://mc-stan.org/cmdstanr/)
- [posterior](https://mc-stan.org/posterior/)
- [bayesplot](https://mc-stan.org/bayesplot/)
- [loo](https://mc-stan.org/loo/)
- [JAX](https://github.com/google/jax)
- [NumPyro](https://num.pyro.ai/en/stable/)
- [Quarto](https://quarto.org/)

## Canonical Textbooks

The most important book-length references for the course are:

- @bernardo1994
- @berger1985
- @gelman2013
- @hoff2009
- @robert2007
- @robert2004
- @brooks2011
- @mcelreath2020
- @mackay2003
- @murphy2023

## Landmark Papers And Articles

Core methodological papers repeatedly used in the course include:

- @metropolis1953
- @hastings1970
- @geman1984
- @andrieu2003
- @neal2011
- @hoffman2014
- @betancourt2017
- @kucukelbir2017
- @blei2017
- @vehtari2017
- @vehtari2021
- @gabry2019
- @ferguson1973
- @neal2000

## Software References

When the course discusses tools or packages, the preferred citations are:

- @r2025
- @carpenter2017
- @cmdstanr2024
- @posterior2024
- @bayesplot2024
- @loo2024
- @jax2018
- @numpyro2020

## Recommended Reading Paths

### Foundations-first

- @durrett2019
- @casella2002
- @bernardo1994
- @jeffreys1961

### Applied modeling

- @gelman2013
- @hoff2009
- @mcelreath2020
- @gabry2019

### Computation-heavy

- @robert2004
- @brooks2011
- @betancourt2017
- @hoffman2014
- @blei2017

## Full Bibliography
